name: 14q12_FOXG1_paralog_region
description: FOXG1 at 14q12 is a haploinsufficient forkhead transcription factor (Rett-like congenital variant). Flanked by paralogous SDs (~5-30 kb at 92-96% identity) shared with related FOX-family loci (FOXA1/FOXC1). Recurrent ~1-4 Mb 14q12 microdeletions and intragenic FOXG1 deletions cause the FOXG1 syndrome.

architecture.internal_structure: Two pairs of directly-oriented SD modules (5-30 kb) at 92-96% identity ~500 kb upstream and ~700 kb downstream of FOXG1; intragenic AluY-AluY pairs additionally drive small deletions.
